senses.definition: A large African tropical tree whose hard wood is used for furniture and building interiors.
examples: The doors were made from durable agba.
Agba is often chosen for high‑quality wooden furniture.
They exported agba for use in European factories.

senses.definition: A written abbreviation for agency, used mainly in lists, tables, and codes.
examples: Look up the agcy code in the system.
Each agcy has its own identification number.
The report groups data by state and agcy.

senses.definition: In ancient Phrygian religion, a powerful deity with both male and female qualities, later linked with the goddess Cybele.
examples: Agdistis was worshipped alongside Attis in Phrygia.
The myth of Agdistis describes a god with a double nature.
Some scholars discuss how Agdistis relates to the Mother Goddess.

senses.definition: The number of years a person has lived or a thing has existed.
examples: What is your age?
The building is over 100 years of age.
Children under five years of age get in free.

senses.definition: A particular period in history or in the development of someone or something.
examples: We live in the digital age.
The Middle Ages were a significant period in European history.
It was an age of great change.

senses.definition: The state of being old or becoming older.
examples: Her back was bent with age.
This wine improves with age.
With age, he became more patient.

senses.definition: To become older or make someone or something appear or become older.
examples: He has aged a lot in the last few years.
Stress can age a person quickly.
The cheese is aged for six months.

senses.definition: Suitable for improving in quality by being kept for a period of time.
examples: This is a highly ageable wine.
Only ageable cheeses are stored in the cellar.
The brewer explained which beers were ageable.

senses.definition: Having lived for a particular number of years.
examples: They have two children aged ten and twelve.
A woman aged 45 was arrested.
The trees, some aged over 200 years, are protected.

senses.definition: Very old.
examples: He looks after his aged parents.
The home provides care for the aged population.
Many aged residents need daily support.

senses.definition: Kept for a period of time so that the taste or quality becomes better.
examples: We ordered a plate of aged cheese.
The restaurant serves aged beef.
Aged wine is stored in oak barrels.

senses.definition: In a way that shows signs of being very old or elderly.
examples: He moved agedly across the room.
She smiled agedly, remembering her youth.

senses.definition: The quality of being old or showing the effects of age.
examples: The painting’s agedness added to its charm.
He was struck by the agedness of the village buildings.

senses.definition: Plural of agedness; different kinds or examples of being old.
examples: The novel explores various agednesses of its characters.
Different agednesses can be seen in the city’s architecture.

senses.definition: Out of the right position or shape; awry.
examples: The door was hanging agee on its hinges.
His hat was knocked agee by the wind.

senses.definition: The process of becoming older.
examples: Scientists study the biology of ageing.
Ageing is a natural part of life.
Healthy habits can slow some effects of ageing.

senses.definition: The act of keeping food or drink for some time so that it develops better taste.
examples: Ageing of cheese takes place in cool rooms.
The ageing of wine in oak barrels adds flavor.

senses.definition: Becoming old or already old.
examples: The country has an ageing population.
They care for ageing parents.
An ageing workforce can affect productivity.

senses.definition: Plural of ageing; different kinds or instances of becoming older or being aged.
examples: Researchers compared ageings in different species.
The book describes various ageings of wine.

senses.definition: Unfair treatment or dislike of someone just because of their age, especially of older people.
examples: She filed a complaint about ageism at work.
Ageism can stop older people from getting jobs.
The campaign fights ageism in the media.

senses.definition: Plural of ageism; different examples or types of unfair treatment because of age.
examples: The report lists various ageisms faced by employees.
They discussed ageisms affecting both young and old people.

senses.definition: Treating people unfairly or disrespectfully because of their age.
examples: That was an ageist comment.
The policy is clearly ageist towards older workers.
He denied making ageist remarks.

senses.definition: A person who treats others unfairly because of their age.
examples: She refused to work for an ageist.
Only an ageist would say older people cannot learn technology.

senses.definition: Plural of ageist; people who treat others unfairly because of their age.
examples: Ageists often assume older workers cannot adapt.
The article criticises ageists in management.

senses.definition: A genus of extinct fossil sea animals related to echinoderms.
examples: Agelacrinites is known from Paleozoic rocks.
The museum has several specimens of Agelacrinites.

senses.definition: A family of extinct fossil echinoderm‑like sea animals that includes Agelacrinites.
examples: Agelacrinitidae are studied for their unusual body structure.
Several genera in Agelacrinitidae come from the same period.

senses.definition: A genus of blackbirds that includes the red‑winged blackbird.
examples: Agelaius is common in North American wetlands.
The best known species in Agelaius is the red‑winged blackbird.

senses.definition: A person who never laughs.
examples: The comedian joked that every audience has at least one agelast.
He was such an agelast that no joke could move him.

senses.definition: Never laughing or unable to laugh.
examples: Her agelastic expression never changed.
The film portrayed an agelastic hero who slowly learned to smile.

senses.definition: Plural of agelast; people who never laugh.
examples: The room was full of agelasts who did not enjoy the show.
Writers often include agelasts to contrast with comic characters.

senses.definition: A variant or misspelling of the genus name Agelaius for blackbirds, not standard in modern usage.
examples: The older paper used the form Agelaus for the genus.
Agelaus is generally corrected to Agelaius in recent texts.

senses.definition: Never seeming to get old.
examples: Her beauty seemed ageless.
He has an ageless face.
The singer has an ageless voice.

senses.definition: Not limited to one time; remaining valuable or true over a long period.
examples: The novel’s themes are ageless.
It is an ageless story of love and loss.

senses.definition: In a way that does not seem to change with age or time.
examples: She remained agelessly elegant.
The song sounds agelessly fresh.

senses.definition: The quality of not seeming to get older or not belonging to one period in time.
examples: She had an air of agelessness.
The design has a certain agelessness.

senses.definition: Plural of agelessness; different examples or types of not seeming to age or be tied to one time.
examples: The essay explores the various agelessnesses of myth.
Different agelessnesses appear in the collection of artworks.

senses.definition: Lasting for a very long time.
examples: They began an agelong journey.
The dispute turned into an agelong conflict.

senses.definition: A person who is the same age as another person.
examples: He feels closer to his agemate than to his brother.
Children often prefer playing with agemates.

senses.definition: People who are the same age as each other.
examples: Children learn social skills from their agemates.
He spends most of his time with agemates.

senses.definition: A non‑standard spelling of against, used in some dialect writing.
examples: He set the ladder agen the wall.
She leaned agen the door, listening carefully.

senses.definition: The name of a bright star in the constellation Centaurus.
examples: Agena is one of the brightest stars in the southern sky.
The navigator used Agena as a reference point.

senses.definition: Plural of agency; businesses or official organizations that provide a particular service.
examples: Several travel agencies offer this tour.
Government agencies must follow strict rules.
Advertising agencies are competing for the account.

senses.definition: A business or organization that provides a particular service for people or other companies.
examples: She works for an advertising agency.
We booked our flights through a travel agency.
The government agency is responsible for public health.

senses.definition: The power or ability of a person to act and make their own choices.
examples: The novel focuses on the heroine’s agency.
Education can give people more agency over their lives.

senses.definition: An incorrect or obsolete form of agenda, rarely used in modern English.
examples: The old minutes list the agend for the next meeting.
Agend appears only in some historical documents.

senses.definition: A list of things to be discussed or done at a meeting.
examples: Let’s look at the first item on the agenda.
The meeting agenda was sent by email.
Climate policy is high on the agenda.

senses.definition: A secret or personal plan that someone wants to follow.
examples: He seemed to have his own agenda.
Some media outlets have a clear agenda.

senses.definition: Without a set list of things to discuss or without a hidden plan.
examples: It was an informal, agendaless meeting.
She claims to be agendaless, just reporting the facts.

senses.definition: Plural of agenda; lists of things to discuss or people’s plans and aims.
examples: Different departments came with their own agendas.
The conference organisers prepared detailed agendas.

senses.definition: A single item that needs to be discussed or done at a meeting.
examples: The main agendum today is budget approval.
Each agendum was considered in turn.

senses.definition: Plural of agendum; individual items to be discussed or done.
examples: Several agendums were postponed.
The chair grouped related agendums together.

senses.definition: A chemical once used to bleach and age flour, now banned in many countries.
examples: Agene was used to whiten flour in the past.
Health concerns led to the removal of agene from food processing.

senses.definition: Plural of agene; separate amounts or uses of that chemical.
examples: The study compared different agenes used in milling.
Old records mention several agenes in processing.

senses.definition: Plural of agenesis; different cases or types of failure of an organ or body part to develop.
examples: The textbook describes several ageneses of the kidney.
Researchers reported rare ageneses in the study.

senses.definition: Failure of an organ or body part to form during development.
examples: The child was diagnosed with renal agenesia.
Agenesia of the corpus callosum can affect brain function.

senses.definition: Plural of agenesia; different cases of failure of an organ or body part to form.
examples: The article reviews several agenesias in newborns.
Different agenesias are classified by affected organs.

senses.definition: Relating to or caused by agenesis, especially absence of an organ.
examples: The patient showed agenesic absence of the left kidney.
Agenesic conditions can be detected before birth.

senses.definition: Failure of an organ or body part to develop during growth.
examples: Agenesis of the kidney was discovered during a scan.
Dental agenesis is common in some populations.

senses.definition: Not caused by genes; not related to genetic factors.
examples: The illness was found to be agenetic.
They focused on agenetic causes of the disorder.

senses.definition: To treat flour with agene to bleach and age it.
examples: Millers used to agenise flour before the ban.
The factory no longer agenises its products.

senses.definition: Treated with agene to bleach and age, especially referring to flour.
examples: Agenised flour was once common in baking.
Health risks led to a ban on agenised products.
